pi_items.source: NIH/NCI publications, NEJM journals, cancer.gov: Lorlatinib is a third-generation ALK inhibitor approved for ALK-positive non-small cell lung cancer
NCBI/PubMed, Mayo Clinic, Drugs.com: Salbutamol is a short-acting beta-2 agonist bronchodilator used for asthma and COPD
NCBI/PubMed, Mayo Clinic, StatPearls: Theophylline is a methylxanthine bronchodilator used for asthma and COPD
NCBI/PubMed, NEJM, DrugBank: Montelukast is a leukotriene receptor antagonist used for asthma and allergic rhinitis
Android standard file path convention for emulated device storage

Liverpool Cancer iChart app evidence screenshot (results_key.png) showing traffic light color system: Red (Do Not Coadminister), Orange (Potential interaction requiring monitoring), Yellow (Potential weak interaction), Green (No clinically significant interaction), Grey (No clear data)

privileged_info_summary: Lorlatinib is a real third-generation ALK inhibitor cancer drug approved for ALK-positive non-small cell lung cancer. The three respiratory co-medications (Salbutamol, Theophylline, Montelukast) are verified as real respiratory medications used for asthma and COPD. The Liverpool Cancer iChart app is a legitimate medical tool developed by the University of Liverpool for checking drug-drug interactions with cancer medications. The traffic light color system (Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Grey) is verified from the app's interface and corresponds to standardized interaction severity ratings from the Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions Database.
